arguments.title: Read files
Read files

content.text:
{
  "buggy": "import re


def sync_block(target_content: str, src_path: str, body: str) -> str:
    """Replace the block between START/END markers for src_path with body.

    NOTE: contains a bug when src_path contains backslashes (e.g. Windows
    paths like C:\\Users\\...), because re.sub interprets backslash escapes
    in the replacement string.
    """
    start_tag = f"<!-- START({src_path}) -->"
    end_tag = f"<!-- END({src_path}) -->"
    pattern = re.compile(re.escape(start_tag) + r"(.*?)" + re.escape(end_tag), re.DOTALL)
    replacement = f"{start_tag}\n{body}\n{end_tag}"
    # Use a function replacement so backslashes in src_path/body (e.g. Windows
    # paths) are not interpreted as regex escape sequences.
    return pattern.sub(replacement, target_content)
",
  "test": "from buggy import sync_block


def test_windows_path_does_not_break_regex():
    src = r"C:\Users\test\module.py"
    target = f"<!-- START({src}) -->\nOLD\n<!-- END({src}) -->"
    result = sync_block(target, src, "NEW DOCS")
    assert "NEW DOCS" in result
    assert "OLD" not in result
    assert f"<!-- START({src}) -->" in result
"
}

item.text: The bug is exactly in the regex replacement: the code builds the right replacement string but passes it directly to `re.sub`, where backslashes are treated as escape sequences. Iâ€™m changing it to a function replacement so the string is inserted literally.
The file is patched. Since PowerShell is still blocked, Iâ€™ll run pytest through the Node runtime if child process execution is available there.

item.text:
Fixed [buggy.py](C:/Users/Administrator/Desktop/cc-bench/codex-run/buggy.py:18) by passing a function to `pattern.sub`, so backslashes in Windows paths are inserted literally instead of being interpreted as regex replacement escapes.

Verification: `python -m pytest -q` passes: `1 passed`. Pytest emitted a cache permission warning for `.pytest_cache`, but the test succeeded.
